title: A resolution designating October 10, 2025, as "American Girls in Sports Day".
plain_summary: This resolution makes October 10, 2025 a special day called American Girls in Sports Day. The day is meant to celebrate women and girls who play sports and their achievements. The Senate wants to recognize Title IX, which is a law that helps make sure girls have equal opportunities in sports. The resolution also asks sports organizations to keep separate teams for biological males and biological females when they compete.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution asks to make October tenth, two thousand twenty-five, a special day called American Girls in Sports Day.

sections.title: Reasons for the Resolution
sections.text: The Senate gives these reasons for creating this special day. Playing sports has important positive effects on young girls. It helps their physical health, builds their confidence, and teaches them discipline. Women have achieved some of the greatest athletic accomplishments in United States sports history, both in the Olympic games and professional competitions. Female athletes have inspired many generations of women and girls. The passage of Title IX in nineteen seventy-two was a turning point moment when the federal government started supporting girls in sports. Title IX is a law that requires equal opportunities for women and men in education programs, including sports. There are basic biological differences between men and women that can put women at a disadvantage in sports and may create safety concerns during competition. In recent years, more biological men have been allowed to compete in women's sports. Since two thousand three, biological men have taken away more than nine hundred fifty championship titles, medals, scholarships, and records from women and girls. This includes at least twenty-eight women's sports titles in volleyball, swimming, mountain biking, track and field, weightlifting, and cycling. The National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics has created new rules to protect biological girls in sports. These rules ensure that only student athletes whose biological sex is female can compete on women's sports teams sponsored by this organization. It is very important to protect women's and girls' opportunities to compete in athletics. October tenth was chosen because when written in Roman numerals as ten-ten, it looks like XX, which represents the female chromosomes.

sections.title: What the Senate Resolves
sections.text: The Senate decides to do four things. First, it recognizes October tenth, two thousand twenty-five, as American Girls in Sports Day. Second, it celebrates how women have impacted sports culture and history in the United States. Third, it recognizes how important Title IX is in protecting biological women in sports. Fourth, it calls on sports governing organizations in the United States and other countries to protect biological women and girls in sports.
